platform.name: Android Developers
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: The selected first-app Compose codelab targets a local Android Studio Preview result and does not require Google Play Console enrollment.
Whether a Google Account is required solely to download Android Studio or sync tooling is not established as part of the codelab's required path.
Basic Kotlin knowledge and Android Studio installation are assumed prerequisites of the selected codelab.

developer_goal: Create the first Kotlin Jetpack Compose app in Android Studio and verify the personalized greeting in Compose Preview.

first_success.outcome: Compose Preview shows a personalized Greeting Card introduction with selected background color and 24.dp padding.
first_success.observable_signal: After padding and imports, Preview renders personalized Hi, my name is ... text on the colored Surface, and the codelab congratulates building the first Compose app.

route_selection.surface: Android Developers codelab: Create your first Android app
route_selection.rule: Use the official first-app Compose codelab after Android Studio install; do not expand into Play Console publishing.
route_selection.selected: Create your first Android app (Compose Preview)

candidates.name: Create your first Android app Compose codelab
candidates.reason: Official bounded first-app codelab ending at personalized Compose Preview.

candidates.name: Google Play Console publisher account path
candidates.reason: Distribution surface after local first-app success; not required by the codelab.

candidates.name: Later text-composables codelab
candidates.reason: Assumes the developer already knows how to create and run an Android Studio app.

required_path.action: Install Android Studio using the official install codelab for the computer's operating system if it is not already installed.
required_path.observable_result: Android Studio launches to the Welcome dialog.

required_path.action: Create a New Project with Empty Activity named Greeting Card, default package and location, Minimum SDK API 24, and Finish.
required_path.observable_result: Project opens after Gradle sync completes.

required_path.action: Update Greeting text and GreetingPreview name, surround Text with Surface, set background Color, and add 24.dp padding with required imports.

required_fields.label: Greeting Text and Preview name edits

required_path.observable_result: MainActivity.kt contains the personalized colored padded greeting composables.

required_path.action: Build and refresh Compose Preview and confirm the personalized greeting card appearance.
required_path.observable_result: Preview shows the personalized introduction; codelab states the first Compose app is built.

prerequisites.description: A computer meeting Android Studio system requirements with internet access.

unavoidable_waits.description: Android Studio install, first project Gradle sync, and Compose Preview build.

platform_outcomes.description: Empty Activity template scaffolds a Compose app with Hello Android starter UI.
Compose Preview renders the edited greeting card.

excluded.item: Running on an emulator or physical device beyond Preview
excluded.reason: Selected codelab terminal is Compose Preview customization.

excluded.item: Google Play Console signup and publishing

excluded.reason: Occurs after the documented first-success statement.

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map when the first-app codelab does not create a Google Play or Android developer console account?
uncertainties.impact: The measured path is local IDE work; forcing an account-creation start either invents a Play Console detour or leaves the contract boundary unmapped.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Policy decision on whether Google Account/Play Console is in-scope for Android Developers first success, plus evidence of any account gate inside Android Studio install.

uncertainties.question: Does current Android Studio still present the same Empty Activity labels and setup screens as the codelab screenshots?
uncertainties.impact: Minor UI differences can change field labels during project creation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated walkthrough on the latest Android Studio confirming template and configuration labels.
